channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: J'Something draws inspiration from real-life encounters for his upcoming single 'I AM'
item.description: South African musician J'Something is taking a deeply personal approach to songwriting with his upcoming single 'I AM'. The artist recently hit the streets to ask strangers the simple yet profound que...

item.title: Trump shares AI-generated sci-fi memes and political commentary on Truth Social
item.description: Following a three-day summit in China, former President Donald Trump spent the weekend in Washington D.C. golfing and actively posting on his social media platform, Truth Social. Among the posts were ...

item.title: Uganda enacts law requiring approval for foreign-backed policy work
item.description: Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni has signed the 'Protection of Sovereignty' bill into law, a measure aimed at curbing foreign influence in domestic affairs. The legislation requires individuals and o...

item.title: NDMA warns of severe heatwave across multiple Pakistani provinces until May 26
item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a high-level alert as severe heatwave conditions are expected to affect large parts of southern and central Pakistan from May 19 to May 26....

item.title: Clarifying Mandates: Ribadu as NSA and the New Homeland Security Adviser Appointment
item.description: This opinion piece wey Yushau A. Shuaib write dey analyse the recent appointment of retired Major General Famadewa as Adviser on Homeland Security under President Bola Tinubu administration. The write...

item.title: Lana Wood Discusses Her Role as Plenty O'Toole in James Bond's 'Diamonds Are Forever'
item.description: Lana Wood, the American actress and younger sister of Natalie Wood, is remembered for her brief but iconic role as Plenty O'Toole in the 1971 James Bond film 'Diamonds Are Forever.' The character of P...

item.title: Investigation launched after woman and two children found dead in Campbelltown residence
item.description: Tragedy struck a home in Campbelltown, in Sydney’s southwest, when police discovered the bodies of a woman and two children on Monday evening. Emergency services were alerted to the residence on Raymo...

item.title: West Bengal Government Constitutes Panels to Probe Corruption and Crimes Against Women During Previous Administration
item.description: The West Bengal government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, has announced the formation of two specialised panels to investigate allegations of institutional corruption and abuse of women duri...

item.title: Tanzania misses World Cup, but questions linger on their recent opponents' prospects
item.description: Tanzania will not be participating in the upcoming World Cup, ending hopes for many local fans who had been following the national team closely. Despite this, attention is shifting to Tanzania’s recen...

item.title: Minnesota GOP Senate candidate previously charged with domestic assault
item.description: Aaron Brutger, a Republican candidate for Minnesota State Senate, has a past legal history that has resurfaced amid his current campaign. In 2009, Brutger, then 26, was arrested and charged with one f...

item.title: Dettol Cool launches #OwnTheSweat 2.0 to help Nigerians stay fresh and hygienic during activities
item.description: For the past couple months, temperatures don dey high for Nigeria, making am hard for people to remain cool and comfortable, especially those wey dey always on the move. To tackle this wahala, Dettol ...

item.title: Senator Padilla Confirms His Vehicle Was Seen Leaving Senate Amid Dela Rosa Incident
item.description: Senator Robin Padilla confirmed on May 18, 2026, that the vehicle seen leaving the Senate premises in the early hours of May 14 belongs to him. During an interview with '24 Oras,' Padilla referred to ...

item.title: Two Police Officers Killed While Escorting Polio Vaccination Teams in Bajaur, KP
item.description: In a tragic development in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Bajaur district, two police personnel lost their lives while escorting polio vaccination teams. The incidents took place on Monday in the Tabbai and Dag...

item.title: Police investigate deaths of three people in Campbelltown home
item.description: On Monday evening, emergency services responded to a residence on Raymond Avenue in Campbelltown, south-west Sydney, following reports of a critical incident. Upon arrival, police and paramedics disco...

item.title: Federal Govt Allows Shops and Eateries to Stay Open Beyond 8pm Until May 31
item.description: The federal government of Pakistan has announced a temporary exemption for markets, shops, and eateries from the previously imposed early closure time of 8pm. This exemption, approved by Prime Ministe...

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Wins Second Consecutive NBA MVP Award
item.description: Oklahoma City Thunder star Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been named the NBA’s Most Valuable Player for the 2025-26 season, marking his second consecutive win. The Canadian guard becomes the 14th player ...

item.title: Phil Gould voices concerns over NSW Blues team selection for Game One
item.description: Former New South Wales coach Phil Gould has expressed his concerns regarding the composition of Laurie Daley’s squad ahead of the first State of Origin game. Gould highlighted potential issues with pl...

item.title: Colombian presidential candidate Abelardo de la Espriella adopts hardline approach inspired by Nayib Bukele
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, an independent presidential candidate in Colombia, is gaining traction among conservative voters by adopting a hardline stance reminiscent of El Salvador's president Nayib Bu...

item.title: Analysis of 2026 World Cup Group D: USA and Turkey favoured to advance
item.description: Group D of the 2026 Fifa World Cup is shaping up to be one of the most competitive sections of the tournament, with pundits describing it as wide open despite clear favourites emerging. The United Sta...

item.title: Janis Paige: Celebrating a Legendary Career in Film and Broadway
item.description: Janis Paige (1922–2024) was a celebrated American actress whose career spanned over six decades, leaving a lasting impact on Hollywood and Broadway. Discovered during World War II while performing at ...
